evals.prompt: I need to start working on ticket DEC-123 for adding user profiles to the dashboard. How do I create the branch?
evals.expected_output: The correct xgit command to create a feature branch: `xgit b -f 123 "add user profiles to dashboard"` (or similar description), resulting in branch name like `feature/DEC-123_add_user_profiles_to_dashboard`.

assertions.name: uses-xgit-b-command
assertions.description: Response uses `xgit b` (not plain git checkout -b)

assertions.name: uses-feature-flag
assertions.description: Response includes the -f flag for a feature branch

evals.prompt: I'm done coding my changes for the login fix. How do I commit them properly using xgit?
evals.expected_output: Guidance to run `xgit c` or `xgit commit`, which launches the interactive conventional commit wizard.

assertions.name: mentions-interactive
assertions.description: Response mentions the interactive/wizard nature of the commit command

assertions.name: no-plain-git-commit
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest plain `git commit -m` as the primary approach

evals.prompt: I want to switch to the branch for ticket 456 but I can't remember the full branch name.
evals.expected_output: Guidance to use `xgit f 456` for fuzzy branch checkout.

assertions.name: uses-xgit-f
assertions.description: Response mentions `xgit f 456` or equivalent fuzzy command

assertions.name: includes-ticket
assertions.description: Response includes the ticket number 456 in the command

evals.prompt: We just started a new repo and I want to configure xgit with our Jira project key ACME. Walk me through setup.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step: run `xgit init` to generate xgitrc.json, then edit the project field to ACME. Also covers other config options like ticketSeparator and descriptorCase.

assertions.name: mentions-project-key
assertions.description: Response explains the project field and how to set it to ACME
